issues.title: letterSpacing in TextField with monospace font is only applied to right side of the first character until a second character is entered, then letterSpacing is applied correctly to both sides
issues.body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Run the sample app here: https://github.com/acoutts/flutter_text_bug/blob/main/lib/main.dart
2. Enter some characters in the text field

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
The letter spacing should be applied to all characters equally no matter the length of the input text.


**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
When only one character is entered, the letterSpacing is only added to the right of the character.
When a second character is entered, then the letter spacing is correctly applied to both sides of the first character.

Video demonstration:
https://giphy.com/gifs/blxgQ70RIoVvJybyW0

While the video demonstrates the behavior on iOS, I tested on an Android device and confirmed the same behavior there.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--

      and attach it below between the lines with the backticks.

      To create it you can use `flutter create bug` command and update the `main.dart` file.

      Alternatively, you can use https://dartpad.dev/
      which is capable of creating and running small Flutter apps.

      Without this we will unlikely be able to progress on the issue, and because of that
      we regretfully will have to close it.
-->

```dart
           TextField(
              style: TextStyle(
                fontFamily: 'NotoMono-Regular',
                fontSize: MediaQuery.of(context).size.width * 0.086,
                letterSpacing: MediaQuery.of(context).size.width * 0.082,
              ),
            )
```

What is supposed to be the behavior here? Is Flutters behavior different from native iOS, Android, Mac, or Chrome?

It seems to be a regression as it didn’t happen in past versions of flutter. 

The expected behavior is if I specify letter spacing, it should be applied equally to all characters no matter the input length.

As demonstrated above, the letter spacing is initially only applied to the right side of the first character if the input length is 1. Only once a second character is added does the letter spacing then get added to the left side of the first character.
Submitted a fix to Skia: https://skia-review.googlesource.com/c/skia/+/541978
I see that one is merged now - when can we expect it to be available in flutter?
Sadly it’s still happening even in the latest beta (3.3).

Correction - it is now fixed! Great news 🙌 
Should we leave this open until it makes it to stable?

issues.title: [iOS/Android] Hot restart while keyboard is up leaves the keyboard up
issues.body:
Fix:

* [x] iOS
* [ ] Android
comments: This is likely related to the question of teaching plugins about hot-restart/hot-reload https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/10437
@wmleler and @LarkAscending  just ran into this.

Not only that, but the left-up keyboard doesn't work (typing on it doesn't put characters into the input field.  In addition, I'm seeing hot reload fail. I am going to add a new bug with instructions on how to duplicate this. So this bug will be redundant. The new bug is #11011

I've updated the title/labelling to clarify that this is an issue on both Android and iOS. The general issue is that the engine itself doesn't currently have any means of cleaning up on hot restarts. A general solution would also include support for signalling plugins that a hot restart has occurred.

Well, for us this is one of the major time wasters - having to fully restart the app (and it's a big one), especially when debugging flows with forms.

This is probably patchable with a little plugin which listens for the hot reload and hides the keyboard if open?  e.g.
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/55281077/how-to-detect-hot-reload-inside-the-code-of-a-flutter-app

I'm not sure if that's the *best* way to fix it, but might be a workaround while other members of the core team are busy with other issues?  Or someone should feel empowered to dive into the framework code an attempt a more sustainable fix?
I suppose, that flutter should try to invoke `dispose()`  before hot restart.

I'm not sure that it's good for each case, but I solved by adding this before super.initState().
```dart 
  SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide');
```

Do we know what is the status of this issue? I have the same problem running Flutter 2.2.3.
Any help ?

@eseidelGoogle do you know the specific detail that's preventing this issue from being resolved?

A hot restart will re-run the entrypoint, which will execute `runApp()`. Is there any reason not to instruct the IME to reset in `runApp()`? Or is there any other aspect of that change that would be a problem?

I have scheduled some time on April 13 12P-3P to tackle this. I would like to get some basic buy in before I start.

Is anyone against me just calling `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide');` or some equivalent in runApp()?

what kinds of tests are expected for this to land?

@maheshj01 can you help me route this?

Drive by comment as I'm ramping up on text input.

> Is anyone against me just calling SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide'); or some equivalent in runApp()?

The engine has a "hot restart" notification ([embedder API](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/c710c160bd29a84479a0f3023c83022fc39bc7b0/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/embedder/embedder.h#L2593-L2601), [Android](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/37e79eb90c744d48a40eed5b0e3404245c324b04/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/embedding/engine/FlutterEngine.java#L138-L147), [iOS](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/37e79eb90c744d48a40eed5b0e3404245c324b04/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/darwin/ios/platform_view_ios.mm#L201-L210)) that embedders should use to reset their state. Could we use that hook to hide the keyboard instead of implementing this on the Dart side?

> what kinds of tests are expected for this to land?

Ideally we'd have an integration test that:

1. Launches an app with a text field
2. Focuses the text field
3. Verifies the keyboard is visible
4. Hot restarts
5. Verifies the keyboard is not visible

issues.title: [Firefox] macOS emoji picker doesn't input an emoji under Firefox to `TextField` due to focus being lost for too long
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Focus into any `TextField` under Firefox browser on macOS device.
2. Press `Control + CMD + Space` or `fn + E` to open up macOS emoji picker.
3. Be sure that the emoji picker is in compact mode (displayed above the field and not floating around) - if not, click on the calendar looking button in the picker so that it shrinks down.
4. Press on any emoji.
5. Notice the emoji not being pasted.

This seems to be happening due to `TextField` losing focus for too long, since if after the 4th step you manually press into the `TextField`, then the emoji is pasted. 

### Expected results

Emoji is pasted into the `TextField` (like it does under any other browser or in macOS application).

### Actual results

Emoji is not pasted.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

I am running this code with the following command: `flutter run -d web-server --web-hostname=0.0.0.0 --web-port=8080` and then open the `http://localhost:8080` URL in Firefox.

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MaterialApp(home: Scaffold(body: Center(child: TextField()))));
}
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d9c6feca-aac3-4c01-b812-090ded678bd9

issues.title: ListView (or basically any scrollable like SingleChildScrollView) with keyboardDismissBehavior set to ScrollViewKeyboardDismissBehavior.onDrag breaks the scrolling on iPad when the finger is inside the TextField
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

Works on iPhone, however, doesn't work on iPad.
How to reproduce:
1. Run the provided code sample;
2. Focus the TextFormField ;
3. Start dragging the ListView by tapping on the TextFormField.

### Expected results

Scrolling shouldn't break

### Actual results

Scrolling breaks.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MainApp());
}

class MainApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MainApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: SafeArea(
          child: ListView(
            padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(
              vertical: 44.0,
              horizontal: 16.0,
            ),
            keyboardDismissBehavior: ScrollViewKeyboardDismissBehavior.onDrag,
            children: [
              TextFormField(
                decoration: const InputDecoration(
                  enabledBorder: OutlineInputBorder(),
                  focusedBorder: OutlineInputBorder(),
                ),
              ),
              const SizedBox(
                height: 20,
              ),
              Container(
                color: Colors.red,
                height: 100,
              ),
            ],
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

```

issues.title: Input methods get interrupted when TextField constraints change
issues.body:
This was originally reported in #120557 but I was told to file this as a separate issue.

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample. Choose **Web** as the platform. <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Switch to Chinese input method. Other input methods may also have this issue but I haven't tested them. <!-- describe steps to demonstrate bug -->
3. Try to compose Chinese characters. You'll see that it is impossible to compose any Chinese character in this TextField. <!-- for example "Tap on X and see a crash" -->

This issue also depends on the input method and the platform. It can be reproduced with the default Chinese Pinyin IME on both Chrome and Safari on macOS (user reports Windows is affected too), but not on iOS.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? --> Expected to be able to compose Chinese characters normally, as in a native text field.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
Input method keeps getting interrupted, making it impossible to compose any Chinese character.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--
      Please create a minimal reproducible sample that shows the problem
      and attach it below between the lines with the backticks.

      To create it you can use `flutter create bug` command and update the `main.dart` file.

      Alternatively, you can use https://dartpad.dev/
      which is capable of creating and running small Flutter apps.

      Without this we will unlikely be able to progress on the issue, and because of that
      we regretfully will have to close it.
-->

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MainApp());
}

class MainApp extends StatefulWidget {
  const MainApp({super.key});

  @override
  State<MainApp> createState() => _MainAppState();
}

class _MainAppState extends State<MainApp> {
  final _controller = TextEditingController();

  bool show = false;

  @override
  void initState() {
    super.initState();
    _controller.addListener(() {
      setState(() {
        show = !show;
      });
    });
  }

  @override
  void dispose() {
    _controller.dispose();
    super.dispose();
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Row(
          children: [
            Expanded(
              child: TextField(
                maxLines: null,
                controller: _controller,
                keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline,
              ),
            ),
            Visibility(
              visible: show,
              child: const IconButton(
                  icon: Icon(Icons.zoom_out_sharp), onPressed: null),
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

comments: It seems that the problem is not related to the PopupMenuButton as it also happens with the TextButton. Maybe the problem is more related to the InputDecoration

Hi @fdemiguel, what are you trying to achieve by placing the `PopupMenuButton` as the label of the `InputDecoration`? 

Also, your flutter doctor shows you're using an older version of flutter. Kindly upgrade to the latest version of flutter to see if you still experience this issue. If you do, please provide the updated output of `flutter doctor -v`. 

Thank you

Hi @danagbemava-nc 

Yesterday I tested it on dartpad (3.19.3) and the same thing happened.

The reason why I want to place a PopupMenuButton as the label of an InputDecoration is that I want to give the user the option to do certain things on a group of elements, in this case, the child of the InputDecoration. Those things would be the items of the PopupMenuButton.

Thank you very much

Hi @fdemiguel, if I understand what you're trying to achieve, I don't think it will be a good user experience for your users. It might be best for you to have a separate field that controls the option you want to apply to the group. 

It would help if you could provide a screenshot of what exactly it is that you want to achieve. 

Thank you

![gb_economic_conditions](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/126063378/070c5612-2138-426a-8614-3da518091f9f)

Hi @danagbemava-nc

PopupMenuButton items are economic entities used in the past by the person. When you select one of them, the data on the screen is updated.

IMO, the "temporary solution" will provide a better experience for your users as most users do not expect the label of an input area to perform an action. You just might want to indicate to them that they would want to click on that area. 

However, it does seem like there's a bug in the popup ink area calculation when it is used in this scenario (and perhaps other similar scenarios). Labeling for further insight from the team.

issues.title: FocusNode change notifier not called when focused TextField is removed
comments: @gspencergoog FYI

This is because if the focus system notified listeners of a focus change after a focus node was no longer in the widget tree, it would often end up calling `setState` on widgets that were unmounted.

I agree that it's not ideal.  Ideally, we'd be able to notify about focus changes synchronously, but that has its own problems: if a node is then simply moved from one place in the tree to another, it would notify about losing and gaining focus, even when it didn't technically do either. Sending focus changes between frames solves this problem, but creates the one you mention above.

I propose an API change wherein the focus change notification includes a reason for the focus change. This reason can specify that focus was lost because the node was unmounted. Client code can then deal with the focus change appropriately, including avoiding a setState() call on an unmounted node.

I can think of a few different ways this API change could be done with a soft migration, if the general approach is approved.
I think in general that it is a good proposal.

issues.title: Flutter form has issues with TextFormField getting focus when voiceover is on
issues.body:
Please refer to the code at https://github.com/mfdcoder/flutter-form-voiceover

Steps to reproduce

1. Switch on Voiceover
2. Using Ctl + Option + Right arrow navigate to the TextFormField
3. Continue navigating to 2nd, 3rd and 4th TextFormField
4. Observe that voice over announces that "You are in a text  field. To enter text in this field type".
5. The input that has focus often does not accept what the user is typing
6. The result is not consistent. The TextFormFields randomly get/don't get focus

The same can be reproduced on the flutter gallery demo app
https://gallery.flutter.dev/#/demo/text-field

Doctor summary (to see all details, run flutter doctor -v):
[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 3.7.0, on macOS 12.5 21G72 darwin-x64, locale en-IN)
[✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 33.0.0)
[✓] Xcode - develop for iOS and macOS (Xcode 14.1)
[✓] Chrome - develop for the web
[✓] Android Studio (version 2021.2)
[✓] VS Code (version 1.75.1)
[✓] Connected device (5 available)
[✓] HTTP Host Availability

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48575544/219421762-0f52f3fa-71a7-4a73-ab3e-c319a04aac17.mov
comments:
Thanks for the detailed report @mfdcoder 
Is the accessibility behavior only occuring on web ? Can you also check the same on mobile platforms (Android or iOS) and see if you still get same behavior or expected results ? That way we can narrow down to platform-specific behavior.
The behaviour is specific to web. Working as expected on iOS and Android

I can reproduce this issue on the latest `stable` and `master` channels with provided sample code. When using `Ctl + Option + Right arrow` to navigate to the next `TextFormField`s, the cursor won't focus on the field sometimes.

But it seems to be related to focus traversal rather than TextFormField (see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/118723). In case using combined keys to navigate between TextFormFields and it doesn't get focus, try going outside the Web and coming back, the focus ring will appear and the TextFormField is also focused. 


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/104349824/220068353-b2b8a107-824b-4b4d-add4-ed12cbd90ee6.mov

Please try this test and confirm if you see the same behavior.
Yes- It works when try going outside the Web and coming back. But we need a solution in which user does not have to go out of the webpage

Thanks for the update. Since this is replicable based on the verification done [here](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120894#issuecomment-1436634496), would be good to get web team's thoughts on the expected behavior.

issues.title: Animation lag when navigating to screen with `TextField` with `autoFocus` set to `true` on iOS
issues.body:
This effects only `iOS`. tested on `iPhone 6` and `iPad (3rd gen)`

##Demo

[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1LPMdNMf6z6yYBCYM3JMegMx-SQw0vGUs/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1LPMdNMf6z6yYBCYM3JMegMx-SQw0vGUs/view?usp=sharing)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. set `autoFocus = true` on first `TextField` in `text_form_field_demo.dart` of Flutter Gallery.

issues.title: Keyboard covering bottom widget after Image Picker select image or cancel

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

add `image_picker: ^1.0.4`

1. Click image select button
2. Select image or click cancel(slide to cancel is normal)
3. Bottom image select button will covering by keyboard
4. Unfocus keyboard and request focus again, it will be normal 

### Expected results

As before select image

### Actual results

Keyboard covering the bottom widget

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(title: const Text("test")),
      body: SafeArea(
        child: Padding(
          padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 16),
          child: Column(
            crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
            children: [
              const Expanded(child: SingleChildScrollView(child: TextField())),
              const Divider(),
              SizedBox(
                  height: 36,
                  child: ListView(
                    scrollDirection: Axis.horizontal,
                    children: [
                      IconButton(
                          onPressed: () async {
                            var picker = ImagePicker();
                            var image = await picker.pickMultiImage();

                          icon: const Icon(Icons.image_outlined))
                    ],
                  ))
            ],
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }

issues.title: AutofillGroup does not autofill all related fields
issues.body:
### Issue Detail

When using AutofillGroup with multiple related input fields, autofill only populates the initially focused field. Other fields within the same AutofillGroup are not automatically filled, even when they have valid autofill hints and are expected to be completed together by the platform.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Save form data on the device (any multi-field form)
2. Open the app and focus on one field
3. Select an autofill suggestion
4. Observe that only the focused field is populated

### Expected results

When selecting an autofill suggestion:

All relevant fields within the same AutofillGroup should be autofilled

Autofill should behave consistently with native platform expectations

### Actual results

Only the currently focused field is autofilled

Other fields inside the same AutofillGroup remain empty

User must manually enter remaining values or click on each field to auto fill remaining fields in same 

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```
AutofillGroup(
  child: Column(
    children: [
      TextFormField(
        autofillHints: const [
          AutofillHints.username,
        ],
        decoration: const InputDecoration(
          labelText: 'Field A',
        ),
      ),
      const SizedBox(height: 16),
      TextFormField(
        autofillHints: const [
          AutofillHints.password,
        ],
        obscureText: true,
        decoration: const InputDecoration(
          labelText: 'Field B',
        ),
        onFieldSubmitted: (_) {
          TextInput.finishAutofillContext();
        },
      ),
    ],
  ),
);

```

</details>

@skaterschikov if you want to give focus to a text field as soon as it’s visible, you can also pass `autofocus: true` to the `TextField`.  Does this work for your use case?

Yes good call pointing out https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/119583, it seems to be the culprit.  I tried this in the browser on some plain HTML and it looks like Flutter's behavior is indeed wrong.  Focusing a field with `document.querySelector('input').focus();` preserves the previous selection.  In Flutter, calling `focusNode.requestFocus();` should probably have the same behavior.

Unfortunately, it will be hard to fix this, because EditableText can't distinguish between receiving focus from `requestFocus` and from tabbing.  @gspencergoog is there any way to distinguish this with plain Focus widgets?

Otherwise, hopefully it's possible to work around this as suggested above.
No, focus from tabbing occurs by calling `requestFocus` on the appropriate focus node, so from the focus node's perspective they are the same.

> Is there a way to check if there is an IME panel opened?

I'm not sure if Flutter or even the browser provide a way to determine this for all cases (like the emoji picker), but for cases where composition is happening (e.g. Japanese keyboard) the browser provides `isComposing` as a property for key events that can tell us we're in the middle of composition.

On the Flutter side try looking at `_currentEditingValue.composing.isValid` to see if composition is happening and if it's valid.  If so, let the event bubble up.

issues.title: [Flutter Web] Rotating the keyboard from landscape to portrait with keyboard open leaves a white area
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce I'm developing a web app with Flutter (version 3.27.0 but had same issue with 3.24). I deployed the app on a web server with flutter build web --release (thus should use canvaskit). Using the web app on a Android (Samsung) device on multiple browsers (Chrome, Firefox and the default Samsung one) I noticed that whenever I rotate the screen from landscape to portrait with the keyboard open I get that the screen space previously used by the keyboard inset turns white and becomes unavailable for the app in a similar way to resizing the screen. Then, the app runs as expected in the remaining part of the screen. When I rotate the screen again, the full screen is used again by the web app. The same issue happens whenever LastPass is automatically opened while writing the username or password (in this case even just staying in portrait). However, I prefer to focus on the rotation part as it is more reproducable. On iOS the issue IS NOT present, probably due to the different rendering engine. This problem happens on any screen which allows to open up the virtual keyboard. ### Expected results When running on Flutter Web on android device (any browser), the app should properly reclaim its space after the keyboard is closed. ### Actual results A white space area occupies the area previously used by the virtual keyboard inset when the app is rotated from landscape to portrait with the virtual keyboard open. ### Code sample
Code sample To verify that the issue is not related to my code I implemented the following basic app with only a Scaffold and a TextFormField, which also presents the aforementioned problem once deployed. ``` dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp(const MyApp()); } class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { const MyApp({super.key}); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp( title: 'Window Bug Test', theme: ThemeData( colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.deepPurple) .copyWith(surface: Colors.black), useMaterial3: true, ), home: const TestScreen(), ); } } class TestScreen extends StatefulWidget { const TestScreen({super.key}); @override State createState() => _TestScreenState(); } class _TestScreenState extends State { final TextEditingController _controller = TextEditingController(); @override void dispose() { _controller.dispose(); super.dispose(); } @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return Scaffold( resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false, body: Center( child: Container( margin: const EdgeInsets.all(16), padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 16, vertical: 4), decoration: BoxDecoration( color: Colors.white, borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(16)), child: TextFormField( controller: _controller, autocorrect: false, enableSuggestions: true, keyboardType: TextInputType.text, maxLines: 8, onSaved: (value) {}, decoration: InputDecoration( hintText: "Add text here", hintStyle: Theme.of(context).textTheme.bodyMedium, alignLabelWithHint: true, border: InputBorder.none, ), style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.bodyMedium, autovalidateMode: AutovalidateMode.onUserInteraction, validator: (value) { if (value!.isEmpty || value.length < 16) { return "Input is too short"; } return null; }, ), ))); } } ```
### Screenshots or Video
Screenshots / Video demonstration Screen looks normal Rotate the screen and open the virtual keyboard Rotate the screen back to portrait -> white area appears. Only goes away when rotating again
### Logs
Logs I could not see logs on the deployed application, but I don't get any error uploaded on Sentry.

Doctor output ```console [✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 3.27.0, on macOS 15.1.1 24B91 darwin-arm64, locale en-IT) • Flutter version 3.27.0 on channel stable at /Users/stefanoconoci/Code/flutter • Upstream repository https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git • Framework revision 8495dee1fd (8 days ago), 2024-12-10 14:23:39 -0800 • Engine revision 83bacfc525 • Dart version 3.6.0 • DevTools version 2.40.2 [✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 35.0.0) • Android SDK at /Users/stefanoconoci/Library/Android/sdk • Platform android-35, build-tools 35.0.0 • Java binary at: /Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home/bin/java • Java version OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 17.0.10+0-17.0.10b1087.21-11609105) • All Android licenses accepted. issues.title: TextField loses focus when scrolling web app
issues.body:

     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

When visiting a Flutter web app from an iPad (or any touch device?), TextFields will lose focus when scrolling. That's a problem, because sometimes only the appearance of the keyboard makes the screen scrollable. The disappearance of the keyboard upon scrolling then makes it impossible to scroll TextFields into view.

This only happens for web apps. When I build the same app for the iPad, the TextField keeps its focus when scrolling.

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run -d chrome` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Visit the web app from an iPad or the iPad simulator
3. Scroll to the input field and focus it to open the keyboard
4. Scroll drag anywhere on the screen to scroll.
5. The text field will lose focus and the keyboard will disappear

**Expected results:** The TextField keeps its focus and the keyboard remains visible.

**Actual results:** The TextField loses focus and the keyboard is dismissed.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--
      Please create a minimal reproducible sample that shows the problem
      and attach it below between the lines with the backticks.

      To create it you can use `flutter create bug` command and update the `main.dart` file.

      Alternatively, you can use https://dartpad.dev/
      which is capable of creating and running small Flutter apps.

      Without this we will unlikely be able to progress on the issue, and because of that
      we regretfully will have to close it.
-->

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      debugShowCheckedModeBanner: false,
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Center(
          child: MyWidget(),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

class MyWidget extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return SingleChildScrollView(
      child: Container(
        padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 20),
        height: 9999,
        child: const TextField(),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

<summary>Flutter doctor</summary>
